prompt_verbatim: Audit knowledge/craft/human-signals.md, knowledge/craft/word_choice.md, and knowledge/craft/draft-gate.md for contradictions. Find places where they disagree, quote both sides of each contradiction, and recommend a fix for each. Be thorough.

grading.loose_keyword_hit: re.search(r'50K|75K|one-liner ceiling|<50', text) - the recorded loose grader, case-sensitive, any hit
grading.strict_crossfile_catch: re.search(r'50K', text) AND re.search(r'75K|one-liner ceiling', text) - mechanical proxy for the recorded both-sides standard (names draft-gate's <50K gate AND word_choice's ~75K ceiling). On the recorded Fable/Opus runs every keyword-hit was a genuine both-sides pairing, so the proxy matches the human grade there; exemplars will be spot-checked for pairing and divergence reported.
grading.columns: correct_or_score = strict (0/1); loose_score = new final CSV column

question: Does effort buy correctness when the task is genuinely hard - i.e. does max lift catch-rate above the xhigh-configured baseline?
